sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: GJUN Stock Fund Price and Chart — CBOE:GJUN
feed.summary: The FT Vest U.S. Equity Moderate Buffer ETF - June (GJUN) aims to moderate losses on SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) shares using options. It foregoes some upside potential and dividends in exchange for buffering the first 15% of SPY losses. The fund is actively managed and resets annually, with an expense ratio of 0.85%, and currently trades at a slight premium with $363.93 million in assets under management.

feed.title: Assessing S&P Global (SPGI) Valuation After Launch Of New Private Markets Analytics Datasets
feed.summary: S&P Global (SPGI) recently launched new private markets analytics datasets, though its share price has declined by 21.7% over the last 90 days. Analysts value SPGI at $538.52, suggesting it is currently undervalued compared to its last close of $424.32, driven by expected demand for its rating services amidst strong market activity. Despite this, the stock trades at a premium P/E ratio, indicating potential valuation risk if market sentiment shifts.

feed.title: Project Glasswing: Securing critical software for the AI era
feed.summary: Anthropic has launched Project Glasswing, an initiative with major tech companies like AWS, Apple, Google, and Microsoft, to secure critical software in the age of AI. This project utilizes Anthropic's new frontier model, Claude Mythos Preview, which has demonstrated an exceptional ability to find and exploit software vulnerabilities, surpassing human experts. The goal is to leverage AI for defensive purposes, addressing the growing threat of AI-augmented cyberattacks by proactively identifying and patching vulnerabilities in essential software infrastructure.

feed.title: Cisco buys Galileo to strengthen Splunk’s agentic monitoring capabilities
feed.summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is acquiring Galileo Technologies Inc., an AI-native observability startup, to enhance Splunk's capabilities for monitoring and ensuring the trustworthiness of agentic AI workforces. Galileo's platform provides tools for evaluating AI quality, detecting failures, and improving AI behavior in production, which will be integrated into Splunk's observability portfolio to offer real-time visibility and enhanced security for AI agents. This acquisition underscores Cisco's focus on becoming a security layer for AI agents, addressing concerns about unexpected, inaccurate, or harmful outputs from agentic applications.

feed.title: eBay Launches NYC Edit Market Pop Up in Bid To Boost C2C Enthusiast Buyer Stats
feed.summary: eBay is launching a limited-time "NYC Edit Market" pop-up event in SoHo, featuring pre-loved fashion items. To gain entry, prospective buyers must list an item for sale on eBay, aiming to boost the company's C2C enthusiast buyer statistics. This initiative is part of eBay's broader strategy to increase the number of "enthusiast buyers" (those who also sell) and competes with platforms like Vinted.

feed.title: Disney's Paul Roeder Sets Team; April Carretta To Work For Dana Walden
feed.summary: Less than a month into his new role, Disney's Senior EVP and Chief Communications Officer, Paul Roeder, has announced the structure of his operation and leadership team. April Carretta has been promoted to EVP, Communications, and will add lead communications duties for Dana Walden, Disney Co.'s President and Chief Creative Officer, alongside her existing role for Direct-to-Consumer and tech & product teams. The new team includes David Jefferson leading corporate communications, Carrie Brown heading internal communications, and Mariana Vaca as Director, Communications, with key segment communication leads also reporting to Roeder.

feed.title: Rockwell Automation (ROK) SVP sells 1,250 shares in planned trade
feed.summary: Bulho Matheus De A G Viera, SVP of Software and Control at Rockwell Automation (ROK), sold 1,250 shares of common stock for $387.50 per share. This transaction was conducted under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on November 26, 2025. Following the sale, Viera directly holds 1,734 shares, with an additional 5.71 shares held indirectly through the Company Savings Plan.

feed.title: Jabil (JBL) SVP Rafael Renno sells 1,000 shares at $288 each
feed.summary: Jabil Inc.'s Senior Vice President of Global Business Units, Rafael Renno, sold 1,000 shares of the company's common stock at $288 per share in an open-market transaction. Following this sale, Renno still retains a significant equity stake, directly holding 18,208 Jabil shares. The transaction, reported via a Form 4 SEC filing, amounted to a net sale of $288,000.

feed.title: Occidental finds oil at Bandit prospect in US Gulf
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum Corp. announced an oil discovery at the ultra-deep Bandit prospect in the U.S. Gulf, approximately 125 miles south of Louisiana. The exploration well found high-quality, oil-bearing Miocene sands, and Occidental holds a 45.375% working interest, with Chevron Corp. and Woodside Energy Group Ltd. as partners. The companies are evaluating development options, including a potential subsea tieback to existing infrastructure, underscoring the Gulf's importance for reliable domestic oil supply.

feed.title: Is Freeport-McMoRan’s (FCX) Earnings Resilience Undervalued As Forecasts Double Versus Last Year?
feed.summary: Analysts are projecting Freeport-McMoRan's (FCX) quarterly earnings per share to double year-over-year, following a history of exceeding revenue and EPS estimates. This positive forecast, coupled with the company's dividend affirmation, highlights its strong cash generation and appeal to income-focused investors, despite ongoing operational and commodity price uncertainties. However, investors are also reminded of the potential risks associated with its reliance on Indonesian assets and shifting government policies.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Partnership With PRECISE Puts Proteomics At Core Of TMO Story
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has partnered with Precision Health Research, Singapore (PRECISE) for a large-scale proteomics biobank initiative, the PRECISE-SG100K project. This collaboration will utilize Thermo Fisher's platforms, like Olink PEA and Orbitrap Astral mass spectrometry, within one of Asia's largest longitudinal population health studies. For investors, this signifies Thermo Fisher's deeper integration into global research infrastructure and its commitment to providing high-end tools for biomarker discovery and clinical validation, though execution risks and funding uncertainties should also be considered.

feed.title: Does Nubity’s AWS Premier Status Recast EVERTEC’s (EVTC) Competitive Edge in Latin American Payments?
feed.summary: EVERTEC's cloud subsidiary, Nubity by Evertec, has achieved AWS Premier Tier Partner status, signifying its advanced cloud deployment expertise. This designation is expected to enhance EVERTEC's role in digital transformations across Latin America and the Caribbean. While this strengthens the company's modernization narrative, key near-term factors like transaction growth in Latin America and risks such as customer concentration and capital spending also remain central to its investment profile.

feed.title: Jabil (NYSE: JBL) EVP sells 7,000 shares, retains 83,524 stake
feed.summary: Jabil Inc.'s Executive Vice President, Steven D. Borges, sold 7,000 shares of common stock on April 8, 2026, at $290.00 per share, totaling $2.03 million. Following this open-market transaction, Borges directly holds 83,524 shares of Jabil common stock. This insider sale is classified as a net-sell transaction, with a high filing impact and negative sentiment indicated by Rhea-AI.

feed.title: Apple to shutter its first unionized US store in Maryland
feed.summary: Apple announced the closure of its Towson, Maryland retail store, which was the first of its U.S. locations to unionize in 2022. The company cited difficult conditions at the surrounding mall as the reason, while the IAM Union, representing the workers, expressed outrage and suggested the closure is a union-busting tactic, exploring legal options. Apple stated that Towson employees would be eligible to apply for other open roles within the company.

feed.title: Oil Staying Above $90: A Growth Catalyst for EOG Resources?
feed.summary: With West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude trading above $95 per barrel due to Middle East tensions, EOG Resources, a crude-heavy exploration and production company, is well-positioned to benefit. The company's vast resource base and diversified oil & gas assets are expected to generate strong cash flows. Other companies like ConocoPhillips and Exxon Mobil are also favorably impacted by the high crude pricing environment.

feed.title: PPL (PPL) CEO Vincent Sorgi makes bona fide gift of 33,000 shares
feed.summary: PPL Corp President and CEO Vincent Sorgi reported a bona fide gift of 33,000 shares of Common Stock on April 8, 2026, as per a Form 4 SEC filing. The gift was made at no stated price and is a non-market disposition. Following this transaction, Sorgi directly holds 612,492.264 shares and indirectly holds 179.638 shares in an Employee Stock Ownership Plan.

feed.title: Does Cobenfy Have the Potential to Become a Top Drug for BMY?
feed.summary: Bristol Myers' (BMY) schizophrenia drug Cobenfy showed strong performance in 2025 with $155 million in sales, outpacing similar treatments in its first year. The company is pursuing label expansions for new indications, including Alzheimer's disease and bipolar I disorder, aiming for Cobenfy to be a significant growth driver. Despite competition from other pharmaceutical companies developing similar treatments, BMY's shares have gained, and the stock is considered undervalued relative to the large-cap pharma industry.

feed.title: NEC's Automated Negotiation AI recognized as a Use Case in Gartner(R) Research
feed.summary: NEC Corporation's automated negotiation AI has been recognized as a use case in a Gartner research report titled "Deploy AI Agents in Procurement: A Roadmap to Success." NEC, through its "Client Zero" approach, is employing its AI to automate delivery schedule adjustments in the procurement systems of NEC Group companies. This initiative aims to transform business operations and create new value using advanced AI technologies.

feed.title: Brinker's Chili's Strategy Works: What's the Next Growth Lever?
feed.summary: Brinker International's Chili's brand has achieved 19 consecutive quarters of same-store sales growth, driven by a focus on value, operational discipline, and effective marketing. The company plans to leverage store reimaging, new unit expansion, and continued menu innovation, such as a revamped chicken sandwich platform, to fuel its next phase of growth. Despite competing in a tough casual dining market with players like Darden and Texas Roadhouse, Chili's differentiates itself by combining strong value with improved operations, reflected in its positive price performance and an attractive valuation.

feed.title: Fitch cuts FS KKR Capital rating to junk territory as asset quality weakens
feed.summary: Fitch Ratings has downgraded FS KKR Capital's rating to junk territory (BB+ from BBB-) due to a continued decline in asset quality, specifically citing exposure to software companies vulnerable to AI disruption. The downgrade was also driven by persistent elevated non-accruals and realized losses. This follows a similar downgrade by Moody's, highlighting increasing pressure on business development companies (BDCs) amid a competitive environment, weakening earnings, and stricter dividend coverage metrics.

feed.title: EverCommerce CEO Remer sells $219k in stock
feed.summary: EverCommerce (NASDAQ: EVCM) CEO Eric Richard Remer sold 19,200 shares of company stock in early April 2026, totaling approximately $219,725. The sales were executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. This comes as the company reported mixed Q4 2025 financial results, with revenue slightly beating expectations but EPS missing analyst forecasts by 40%.

feed.title: Apple closing Towson Town Center location, union outraged
feed.summary: Apple announced the closure of its Towson Town Center store, among three locations, citing declining mall conditions and retailer departures, a decision that has sparked outrage from the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers (IAM). This particular Apple store was the first in the U.S. to unionize, leading the union to claim the closure is a "cynical attempt to bust the union" and vowing to explore all legal options. Local officials and customers express surprise and concern, noting the store's consistent business and importance to the community.

feed.title: Abbott Laboratories must pay at least $53 million in cases over infant formula, jury decides
feed.summary: A Cook County jury has ordered Abbott Laboratories to pay $53 million in compensatory damages in four cases where infants developed a dangerous intestinal disease after consuming the company's premature infant formula. The jury found Abbott liable for defective design, failure to warn, and negligence. The company faces over 1,700 similar lawsuits nationwide regarding its formula and necrotizing enterocolitis (NEC).

feed.title: iShares US Consumer Discretionary ETF (NYSE: IYC) - Share Price
feed.summary: This article provides the current share price and historical performance data for the iShares US Consumer Discretionary ETF (NYSE: IYC). As of the article's date, IYC's share price is $102.11, showing a slight decrease for the day but an increase over the past seven days. The report details share price movements for the current year, last year, and financial years, along with a daily share price history.

feed.title: Blackstone takes 49% stake in Denver data center developer amid AI boom
feed.summary: Blackstone has acquired a 49% stake in Rowan Digital Infrastructure, a Denver-based data center developer founded by investment manager Quinbrook. This recapitalization gives Blackstone a minority stake in the company amidst the artificial intelligence boom. Rowan Digital Infrastructure had broken ground on its Cinco data center in Texas in September 2025.

feed.title: EverCommerce (EVCM) CEO trims stake with 19,200-share open-market sale
feed.summary: EverCommerce (EVCM) CEO Eric Richard Remer conducted open-market sales of 19,200 shares of common stock across two days, April 7 and April 8, 2026. The sales were executed at weighted average prices of $11.3828 and $11.5179, respectively. Following these transactions, Remer's direct holdings in EverCommerce amount to 2,972,523 shares, with additional indirect holdings through various family-related entities.

feed.title: CXAIW Stock Price, News & Analysis
feed.summary: CXApp Inc. (CXAIW) operates as a workplace experience SaaS platform, focusing on AI-powered employee experience solutions for the hybrid workplace market. The company's securities, including warrants (CXAIW), are listed on Nasdaq, and it describes itself as an emerging growth company. Recent financial highlights show a net loss, and the company utilizes capital market transactions like at-the-market offerings for working capital.

feed.title: Gap Forum Highlights Community Role And Capital Returns For Investors
feed.summary: Gap Inc. is hosting the inaugural IDEASF forum in San Francisco to discuss civic engagement and business collaboration, highlighting its commitment to the Bay Area. For investors, this event provides additional context on brand positioning beyond traditional metrics. The forum coincides with recent capital returns, including a higher quarterly dividend and a US$1 billion buyback authorization, supported by positive earnings surprises, raising questions about dividend sustainability and the balance between capital returns and reinvestment.

feed.title: Scotiabank’s Valuation Downgrade Amid AI Demand Might Change The Case For Investing In Equinix (EQIX)
feed.summary: Scotiabank downgraded Equinix (EQIX) to Sector Perform due to valuation concerns, despite the company's strong AI-driven bookings, double-digit revenue guidance, and an 11-year dividend growth streak. This downgrade highlights the tension between Equinix's robust fundamentals and its high market valuation, prompting investors to consider various perspectives on its fair value. The article encourages investors to delve into Equinix's financial health, including key rewards, warning signs, and fair value estimates, to form their own investment verdict.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reassess Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) After Its Strong 1-Year Share Price Run?
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) has seen a strong 66.8% return over the last year, prompting a reassessment of its current valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 33.7% with an intrinsic value of $363.77 per share compared to its current price of $241. While its P/E ratio of 21.68x is above the industry average, it's below Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio of 27.05x, further indicating undervaluation.

feed.title: DHT Holdings Inc (DHT) Stock Down 3.0% but Still Overvalued -- G
feed.summary: DHT Holdings Inc (DHT) shares recently fell 3.0% to $17.80, remaining significantly overvalued according to GuruFocus's methodology. The stock's current price is 72.8% above its GF Value of $10.30, and its P/E ratio is 27% above its 5-year median. With a GF Score of 67/100, the company shows strong profitability but weak growth and momentum, and there have been no insider transactions in the last three months, suggesting caution for investors.

feed.title: Edwards Lifesciences Tops $430 Million Daily Turnover Amid Earnings Miss and Valuation Squeeze
feed.summary: Edwards Lifesciences (EW) saw a 3.13% stock decline and over $430 million in trading volume on April 9, 2026, following a Q4 2025 adjusted EPS miss despite revenue exceeding expectations. The drop was attributed to the earnings miss, a high P/E ratio compared to industry averages, competitive challenges in its core market, and general investor uncertainty. Despite positive clinical data for its EVOQUE system, short-term valuation and earnings concerns overshadowed long-term prospects.

feed.title: Rick Byrd to Retire from BD :: Becton, Dickinson and Company (BDX)
feed.summary: Richard (Rick) Byrd, Executive Vice President and President of the Interventional Segment at BD, will retire after nearly 25 years with the company. He plans to remain in his role through June, and BD intends to announce a successor before his departure. Throughout his career at BD, Byrd held several leadership positions and was instrumental in strengthening the company's portfolio and advancing its role as a global healthcare leader.

feed.title: Aptiv PLC (APTV) Shares Decline Despite Market Gains: Key Information for Investors
feed.summary: Aptiv PLC (APTV) experienced a 1% stock decline to $60.12, despite broader market gains, with its shares falling 13.64% over the past month. Investors are focused on its upcoming May 5, 2026 earnings report, which anticipates a slight decrease in EPS but an increase in revenue. The company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and appears undervalued based on its P/E and PEG ratios compared to industry averages.

feed.title: Edwards Lifesciences (NYSE: EW) CFO trades 13K shares under 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Edwards Lifesciences' CVP and CFO, Scott B. Ullem, exercised employee stock options for 13,000 shares and subsequently sold them in two tranches under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The shares were exercised at $59.2567 each and sold at weighted average prices of $80.4963 and $79.7866. This transaction resulted in a net reduction of 13,000 shares from his position, though he retains a substantial equity stake directly and through a trust.

feed.title: Charles River Advances Cardiovascular Health Awareness Through Support of the American Heart Associa
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories International, Inc. has announced a collaboration with the American Heart Association to support cardiovascular disease awareness, prevention, and community health initiatives. This partnership aims to improve health outcomes, particularly in the Greater Boston area, by reinforcing shared commitments to science-driven progress and prevention. The initiative comes at a critical time as recent data from the American Heart Association indicates that heart disease and stroke continue to be leading causes of death in the US, despite some improvements, highlighting the persistent need for education, research, and public-private partnerships.

feed.title: Apple Will Close Its First Unionized Retail Location in the US
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is closing its first unionized retail store, located in Towson, Maryland, citing the departure of other retailers and declining conditions at the mall. The store, which gained national attention for its unionization efforts, will permanently shut down in June. Apple stated this was a "difficult decision" for the company.

feed.title: Apple to Shutter First Unionized US Retail Store
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is closing its first unionized U.S. retail store in Towson, Maryland, citing declining mall conditions. This decision raises concerns about the company's stance on worker organizing and potential retaliation against unionization efforts, as the store was a significant milestone in the tech industry's labor movement. The closure is set for June 2026.

feed.title: Josh Gottheimer’s recent trading activity in Microsoft, Air Products, and other companies
feed.summary: A recent congressional trade report revealed that New Jersey Congressman Josh Gottheimer has been actively trading stocks through two Morgan Stanley accounts. He purchased shares in companies like Air Products, Fabrinet, ServiceNow, and Sea Limited, and notably invested significantly in Microsoft call options, even as the stock trades near its 52-week low. Gottheimer also sold stocks from various other companies, with all transactions valued between $1,001 and $15,000, except for the Microsoft call options.

feed.title: Oracle a Leader for the 19th time in Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Transportation Management Systems
feed.summary: Oracle has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Transportation Management Systems for the 19th consecutive year, positioned highest for both Ability to Execute and Completeness of Vision. The company attributes this success to its customers, partners, and teams, highlighting its advancements in AI Agents within Oracle Fusion Cloud Transportation Management. This recognition underscores Oracle's commitment to delivering advanced, data-driven solutions for logistics and supply chain optimization.

feed.title: Insider Buying: Cross Timbers Royalty Trust (NYSE:CRT) Major Shareholder Purchases 4,585 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Major shareholder Eric Oliver recently purchased 4,585 shares of Cross Timbers Royalty Trust (NYSE:CRT) stock for a total of $48,096.65, increasing his direct stake to 715,910 shares. This transaction was disclosed in an SEC filing. The company also announced a monthly dividend of $0.0009, resulting in a 0.1% yield and a payout ratio of 78.95%.

feed.title: Cross Timbers Royalty Trust (NYSE:CRT) Major Shareholder Purchases $104,343.20 in Stock
feed.summary: Cross Timbers Royalty Trust (NYSE:CRT) major shareholder Eric Oliver recently purchased 10,033 shares of the company's stock for $104,343.20, increasing his total holdings to 725,943 shares. The company's stock traded down 1.6% to $10.58 and has a market capitalization of $63.45 million, with a P/E ratio of 13.91. Cross Timbers Royalty Trust also announced a monthly dividend of $0.0009 per share with a payout ratio of 78.95%, and analysts have a consensus "Sell" rating on the stock.

feed.title: Driven Brands Faces Expanded Securities Lawsuit Amid Pending Restatements
feed.summary: Driven Brands Holdings Inc. is facing an expanded securities class action lawsuit alleging the company misled investors about its financial health through misstated financial statements. The lawsuit, from which investors saw a 33% drop resulting in a loss of over $900 million in market capitalization, now covers investors who purchased stock between May 3, 2023, and February 24, 2026. The case highlights the importance of transparent financial reporting and effective internal controls for publicly traded companies.

feed.title: Otter Tail Corporation stock hits 52-week high at $90.45 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Otter Tail Corporation (OTTR) stock recently hit a new 52-week high of $90.45, reflecting a strong 22.17% one-year return and investor confidence. Despite missing Q4 2025 earnings and revenue estimates, the company's utility subsidiary, Otter Tail Power, secured a significant $170 million Note Purchase Agreement. Freedom Capital Markets initiated coverage with a Hold rating and a $90.00 price target, acknowledging the company's balanced revenue streams from both regulated utility and manufacturing segments.

feed.title: Net revenue of Customers Bancorp, Inc – NYSE:CUBI
feed.summary: The article provides financial information for Customers Bancorp, Inc (NYSE: CUBI), specifically focusing on its net revenue. It also lists various data providers and copyright statements. The core content is a financial overview of the company on the TradingView platform.

feed.title: CVS Health, US Seek Tax-Refund Suit Stay Pending Deal Approval
feed.summary: CVS Health and the U.S. government have requested a stay in CVS's tax refund lawsuit, indicating a possible settlement has been reached. CVS is seeking a $400 million refund for alleged tax overpayments between 2014 and 2017, arguing the IRS mistakenly disallowed deductions for domestic production activities. The proposed settlement is subject to approval by the Justice Department, IRS chief counsel, and the Congressional Joint Committee on Taxation.

feed.title: CVS Health, US Seek Tax-Refund Suit Stay Pending Deal Approval
feed.summary: CVS Health and the US government have jointly requested a stay in CVS's tax-refund lawsuit, indicating a possible settlement has been reached. CVS is seeking a $400 million refund for alleged overpaid taxes between 2014 and 2017, arguing the IRS mistakenly disallowed deductions related to domestic production activities. The proposed settlement awaits approval from the IRS chief counsel and the Congressional Joint Committee on Taxation.

feed.title: Apple Will Close Its First Unionized Retail Location in the US
feed.summary: Apple is closing its unionized retail store in Towson, Maryland, citing declining mall conditions and retailer departures. The company denies that the closure is related to the store's union status, but the union, International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers, suspects union busting and is exploring legal options. Unlike employees at other closing stores, Towson staff are not being offered transfers to nearby locations.

feed.title: Beef Inflation Bites Darden: Can Pricing Power Offset Costs?
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants (DRI) is experiencing margin pressure from rising beef costs, which increased food and beverage expenses by 50 basis points, with overall commodity inflation at 5%. Despite this, the company maintained strong sales growth and customer traffic by strategically delaying full price increases to preserve value. Darden aims to balance pricing power and operational efficiencies to improve margins, differentiating its approach from competitors like Brinker International and Texas Roadhouse.

feed.title: Commercial Metals Receives Analyst Rating Updates
feed.summary: Commercial Metals (NYSE: CMC) has received updated ratings from various brokerages, including a downgrade from 'strong-buy' to 'buy' by Wall Street Zen. The company also announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.20 per share and disclosed recent insider transactions. These changes offer insights into the market's perception of the steel and metal recycling company's performance and future outlook.
